notes: The versioned policy governing deterministic candidate generation (PRD 026 §7-8). Given identical evidence versions, ontology version, policy version, constraints, and preferences, rounds must be identical.

All matcher thresholds are INITIAL CALIBRATION — spike-derived placeholders pending pilot evidence. They are versioned policy, not validated constants; changing any threshold requires a new policy version.

Matcher semantics (PRD 026 §7): +1 = verified evidence aligns with the positive statement; -1 = verified evidence supports its defined opposite; 0 = unknown, unsupported, missing, suppressed, or inside the neutral band. Absence NEVER means mismatch.
A preference key with no matcher entry is unsupported: it contributes zero to relevance and stays visible in the ledger (reflection-only keys are listed in unsupported_keys).

Matchers whose evidence_keys reference sources not yet loaded (ipeds.ic.*, distance.metro_proximity, derived cds metrics) are defined but return 0 until the load ships — 'supported preference' additionally requires at least one usable evidence fact at runtime.
Reference implementation: tools/discovery/data_spike.py executes this file; tools/discovery/test_policy.py pins its invariants.

eligibility.description: Stage 1 predicate — the exact meaning of 'in scope' (PRD 026 §7).

predicates.note: exactly one directory row per ipeds_unitid

academic_plausibility.description: Stage 3: at least one positive bachelor's award in the selected IPEDS collection cycle for a direct or adjacent reviewed edge. Exploratory edges never gate. Zero or missing counts are unknown, never negative evidence.

scoring.essential_threshold_note: abs(aggregate) >= 3 is treated as essential-strength for contrast-slot protection; any other nonzero aggregate is interesting-strength.

life.weekend_vitality.note: proxy shared with residential_campus until a better signal exists

spirit.faith_life.note: pending IPEDS IC load; no opposite — secular schools host faith communities, so unaffiliated is 0, never -1

people.background_diversity.derived_method: b2_diversity_index_v1 (pending — definition requires its own review)

cost.merit_aid.note: partial/limited/missing are 0 — thin merit data is unknown, not verified absence

unsupported_keys.description: Reflection-only preference keys: no matcher, zero relevance contribution, always visible in the ledger.

slots.rule: strongest direct evidence match (highest relevance among direct-path candidates)
highest related-CIP count among remaining direct matches, counted within the selected concepts' direct+adjacent edge sets
highest relevance among candidates mismatching exactly one supported interesting-strength preference and mismatching no essential-strength (abs(aggregate)>=3) preference

lowest non-missing scorecard_summary.avg_net_price among remaining academically plausible candidates; NPT4_PUB for public, NPT4_PRIV for private; missing values cannot fill the slot; display the federal metric's population and year; never call it the student's price
highest relevance outside preferred radius (and inside maximum when one exists); only when the student allowed wildcards and a preferred radius exists
highest remaining relevance after diversity constraints

diversity.note: applied during each slot selection when the eligible pool contains alternatives

relaxation.trigger: diversity caps prevent the four-school minimum
relaxation.record: relaxation level and schools added per level in round diagnostics

cooldowns.not_for_me: never repeats in the current session
cooldowns.readmission: oldest unreacted only, labeled Revisit, never saved or rejected schools

tpl.academic_direct.v1.text: Reported bachelor's degrees in {program_label} in {data_year} — a direct path for your interest in {concept_label}.

tpl.academic_adjacent.v1.text: Reported bachelor's degrees in {program_label} in {data_year} — a related path for your interest in {concept_label}.

tpl.offering.v1.text: Reports offering {offering_label} in its Common Data Set ({data_year}).

tpl.numeric_high.v1.text: Reported {metric_label} of {value} in {data_year} — in line with what you said matters.

tpl.numeric_low_good.v1.text: Reported {metric_label} of {value} in {data_year} — on the lower side, which matches what you asked for.

tpl.category.v1.text: Federal data classifies this campus setting as {category_label} ({data_year}).

tpl.distance.v1.text: About {miles} miles from your starting point (straight-line, not driving time).

tpl.affordability_slot.v1.text: Average net price {value} for {population} in {data_year} — shown for cost context, never a prediction of your family's price.

reason_templates.note: Template placeholders resolve from evidence facts at render time; a reason missing a valid template, evidence, or limitation version is omitted (fail closed, PRD 026 §9). Each matcher's limitation_id overrides the template default when they differ.
